key_pattern: Using deadline extensions and reinforcement to preserve leverage
main_constraint: Oil-market and Gulf-base exposure raised the cost of immediate maximal escalation
main_incentive: Preserve pressure on Iran while avoiding a full Hormuz energy shock

key_pattern: Broadening target categories faster than diplomacy can constrain them
main_constraint: Reliance on U.S. support and continued inbound fire limited total autonomy
main_incentive: Exploit wartime momentum to degrade Iranian military and nuclear-linked capacity

key_pattern: Combining selective maritime control with retaliatory signaling
main_constraint: Risk of wider coalition intervention limited formal total closure
main_incentive: Raise U.S. and partner costs without publicly capitulating under fire

key_pattern: Maintaining an active-bounded front that keeps pressure on Israel
main_constraint: Heavy Israeli retaliation and Lebanese civilian costs constrained expansion
main_incentive: Preserve deterrent relevance and support Iran without triggering all-out surge

key_pattern: Remaining a latent escalation reserve rather than the lead widening front
main_constraint: Retaliation risk and Iraqi domestic limits discouraged overt activation
main_incentive: Retain leverage against U.S. assets while avoiding immediate main-front exposure

key_pattern: Signaling latent maritime entry conditions while Hormuz remains the main coercive lane
main_constraint: Counterstrikes and role competition with Hormuz limited immediate activation
main_incentive: Exploit sea-lane vulnerability if the conflict widens into a broader maritime campaign
